title: Series LLC Operating Agreement
description: Operating agreement establishing parent Series LLC structure, separating assets and liabilities between independent cells.
whatsCovered: Establishment of separate series cell networks
Liability segregation: cell assets are protected from other cell debts
Manager allocation for parent LLC and individual series
Capital contributions and profit splits per series
Creation procedure for adding new series cells
whenToUse: Use this Series LLC agreement (e.g. in Delaware, Texas, Wyoming) to hold multiple distinct properties or businesses under separate liability cells.

fields.label: First Series Cell Name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Series A - 123 Main St Property

clauses.heading: SERIES LLC OPERATING AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Series LLC Operating Agreement. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. Series Cell Isolation
clauses.body: Company establishes a Series LLC structure. The initial liability cell is named: {initial_series_name}. Cell assets are isolated from parent liabilities.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
